title: prostanthera ringens
intro: Prostanthera ringens, also known as Tasmanian native rose or Native rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to the south-eastern regions of Australia, particularly in Tasmania, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Prostanthera ringens can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, allowing for a convenient and accessible way to harness its medicinal properties.
